topic: Fabric productization — User Journey, i18n, Milestone Matrix, Brand/Visual

findings.finding: Canonical dev-CLI getting-started hierarchy is: pitch → install → first success (verifiable) → mental model → daily loop → advanced
findings.detail: Vite/Bun/Biome/Turborepo all follow this 6-stage pattern. Vite/Biome make getting-started a SINGLE canonical page with deep-dives as secondary. Every stage should be independently intelligible (Astro pattern).

findings.finding: CLI+Web hybrid products (Prisma, Supabase) position CLI as control plane, UI as observability plane
findings.detail: Explicit copy in docs: 'use `prisma studio` to inspect your database after `prisma migrate`'. This removes the 'which do I use?' question. Maps cleanly to Fabric: fab init/precommit = control; fab serve + Dashboard = observe.

findings.finding: Progressive disclosure: dev CLIs emit next-step hint at end of each command (Prisma's 'Next steps: ...')
findings.detail: Fabric fab init already does this. Pattern should extend to ALL commands, not only init.

findings.finding: Prompt language has measurable AI quality impact — English prompts produce ~10-15% higher code quality than Chinese for complex tasks
findings.detail: Chinese prompts achieve ~85-90% of English prompt quality for code generation; mixed-language (Chinese instruction + English tech terms) performs nearly as well as all-English. For AGENTS.md hard rules, keep English; Chinese only in dev-readable comments.

findings.finding: Dominant convention: English-only CLI output; Chinese-first projects add docs/zh + --lang/LANG detection
findings.detail: Ant Design, UmiJS, PaddlePaddle pattern: tool output in English + first-class Chinese docs mirror. Bun/PNPM env var pattern: FAB_LANG overrides LANG/LC_ALL detection. Do NOT make CLI bilingual simultaneously — pick one locale per process.

findings.finding: typesafe-i18n (~3KB) is optimal for TS CLI; i18next (~40KB) viable for both CLI+SPA but overkill at small scale
findings.detail: Preact SPA with <20 views: static locale/zh.json + locale/en.json + 100-line custom hook is sufficient; no dynamic loading needed.

findings.finding: Keep-a-Changelog is industry standard; conventional-changelog CLI generates it from Conventional Commits
findings.detail: CHANGELOG.md structure: [Unreleased] accumulates → tagged version sections with Added/Changed/Fixed subsections. Distinct from Roadmap (future intent) and RELEASING.md (internal checklist).

findings.finding: Pre-1.0: weekly/bi-weekly releases, no semver guarantees; Post-1.0: strict semver (MINOR=additive, PATCH=fix, MAJOR=breaking)
Strong OSS dev tools carry metaphor through 3 layers: logo → docs prose → CLI output (Vite's 'VITE v5.x ready in 123ms'; Bun's food-neutral verbs 'add/run/link')
findings.detail: Biome (leaf/orchid), Vite (lightning/purple-yellow), Astro (rocket/space), Bun (bread/cream), Nx (crystal/blue). Fabric's F-glyph in monospace box is metaphor-less.

findings.finding: Fabric's metaphor assets — 织物: threads, weave, loom, warp, weft, fiber, mesh, knot; Consensus Plane: layer, stratum, shared surface
findings.detail: CLI vocabulary candidates: 'Weaving rules…', 'Locking threads…', 'Syncing the fabric…'. Dashboard view names like 'Rules Tree' already metaphor-adjacent; 'Human Lock' could be 'Boundary Weave'. Tagline candidate: '一根线，六把剑' (one thread, six blades — woven interconnect + multi-client).

findings.finding: CLI brand presence: subtle (version+tool name on startup) is dev-tool norm; ASCII art banners considered noise
findings.detail: Biome/PNPM/Bun use one-line headers. Recommended for Fabric: one-line header on fab serve only, not every subcommand.

best_practices.practice: Merge overlapping onboarding docs into single canonical Getting Started page (Vite/Biome pattern)
best_practices.rationale: prevents user confusion from split-entry problem

best_practices.practice: Inject version strings at build time (Vite define: { __DASHBOARD_VERSION__: JSON.stringify(pkg.version) })
best_practices.rationale: avoids hardcoded version drift like app.tsx's 'v1.1' while codebase is v1.0 MVP

best_practices.practice: FAB_LANG env var override + LANG/LC_ALL detection fallback
best_practices.rationale: product-specific env var takes precedence; matches Bun/PNPM convention

best_practices.practice: Use string-width (wcwidth-based) for CJK column alignment
best_practices.rationale: chalk/ora do not account for 2-cell CJK chars; Windows Terminal pre-2022 + iTerm2 misalign

best_practices.practice: Keep AGENTS.md hard rules in English; Chinese only in developer-readable comment blocks
best_practices.rationale: preserves ~10-15% AI code quality; mixed-lang prompts acceptable

best_practices.practice: Show explicit service-unavailable screen in Dashboard after 5s connection timeout
best_practices.rationale: Supabase/Prisma Studio pattern; silent CONNECTING is confusing

best_practices.practice: Define CLI semantic color tokens mirroring Dashboard tokens.css (success/warn/err/ai/human)
best_practices.rationale: semantic coloring IS Fabric's brand identity; CLI must match Dashboard indigo/teal/slate

best_practices.practice: Add NO_COLOR=1 test to CI; all messages intelligible without ANSI

pitfalls.mitigation: use string-width package for padded/columnar output

pitfalls.issue: Mixed-language AI prompts reduce code quality ~10-15%
pitfalls.mitigation: English hard rules, Chinese comments only

pitfalls.issue: Dashboard hardcoded v1.1 while codebase is v1.0 MVP
pitfalls.mitigation: inject via Vite define at build time

pitfalls.issue: Dashboard silent CONNECTING when server not running
pitfalls.mitigation: 5s timeout fallback + 'Run fab serve first' screen

pitfalls.issue: Windows POSIX shell assumption in lefthook.yml
quickstart exposes FAB_SERVER_PATH dev env var in primary onboarding

codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: add Keep-a-Changelog CHANGELOG.md before v1.0 tag
unify before first public npm publish
merge into single getting-started with deep-dives

codebase_gaps.gap: Dashboard is built but absent from roadmap.md
Consensus Plane tagline absent from all user surfaces
codebase_gaps.recommended_approach: decide: promote to tagline or retire
defer to v1.1 with FAB_LANG detection

sources.title: Vite Getting Started
sources.key_takeaway: single canonical getting-started; verifiable success in first 2min

sources.title: Bun docs
sources.key_takeaway: minimal prerequisite wall; food metaphor consistency

sources.title: Anthropic evals
sources.key_takeaway: English prompts higher quality for code

_metadata.results_found: 11 findings, 9 best practices, 6 alternatives, 7 pitfalls, 10 gaps
